title_guess: Day trip to Hanbury Hall (near Droitwich, Worcestershire)

pages.text: went on a day trip to Hanbury Hall 1801 near Droitwich. It's very attractiv[e] with wrought iron gates + lovely views over Worcestershire, near the Malverns. We saw inside an ice house,

pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn cross-section diagram of an icehouse structure. Labels include: 'hatch to shovel ice + Snow' on the left side of the structure, 'passage to take out ice' with an arrow pointing to the right passage, and 'drain to ice pond' at the base with a curved arrow. The sketch shows a domed/rounded chamber with a hatch entrance on the left and a horizontal passage on the right.
pages.text:
the icehouse was 6m wide & high
     built of brick.

hatch                 passage to take out ice.
to shovel [diagram of icehouse structure]
ice +
Snow          drain to ice pond

There was also a mushroom house
orchard, orangerie.

captions: Hanbury Hall parterre. 2002, NT & Nick Moss
